picks.rationale: Valero has surged +85% over 1Y and +63.74% YTD, with +2.41% 1M momentum, a P/E of 19.57, and a 1.79% dividend yield, while benefiting from record diesel crack spreads of $86.25/barrel due to the Strait of Hormuz disruption [ainvest.com](https://www.ainvest.com/news/refiner-windfall-narrative-hidden-expiration-date-2607/).

Marathon Petroleum delivered +51% 1Y and +62.71% YTD returns, with +5.04% 1W momentum, a P/E of 17.55, and a 1.47% dividend yield, supported by Q1 earnings that beat consensus by 50% amid elevated refining margins [ainvest.com](https://www.ainvest.com/news/refiner-windfall-narrative-hidden-expiration-date-2607/).

EOG Resources has gained +24.03% YTD with a P/E of 12.86, a 3.12% dividend yield, and positive news sentiment of 0.289, while maintaining a low-cost production base that is resilient in a $65-$75 Brent range [getfinancebrief.com](https://getfinancebrief.com/story/bessent-iran-oil-china-buyer-markets).
SSE has risen +28% over 1Y and +10.26% YTD, with strong 1M momentum of +9.04% and a 2.88% dividend yield, as a regulated utility with growing renewables exposure and a P/E of 23.43.

NextEra Energy posted +20% 1Y and +10.71% YTD returns, with +5.21% 1M momentum, a P/E of 22.42, a 2.82% dividend yield, and near-zero short interest of 0.01%, reflecting strong demand for clean power generation.

Chevron has gained +14% 1Y and +10.60% YTD, with a 4.21% dividend yield, a P/E of 29.48, and positive news sentiment of 0.304, while benefiting from a floor under oil prices as Iran exports remain sanctioned [getfinancebrief.com](https://getfinancebrief.com/story/bessent-iran-oil-china-buyer-markets).
drivers: oil price floor from Iran sanctions

picks.rationale: ExxonMobil is up +22% 1Y and +13.28% YTD, with a 3.01% dividend yield and a P/E of 23.08, while its Rovuma LNG project in Mozambique could add ~$11B annually to the local economy once production begins [fxleaders.com](https://www.fxleaders.com/news/2026/07/02/exxon-mobil-stock-tests-key-support-as-oil-outlook-and-texas-move-take-focus/).

Shell trades at a P/E of 12.15 with a 4.01% dividend yield, +8% 1Y return, and positive news sentiment of 0.23, while its upcoming earnings are expected to show EPS growth of 76.76% year-over-year [wallstreetwaves.com](https://wallstreetwaves.com/understanding-shells-underperformance-compared-to-the-broader-market/).

TotalEnergies has returned +22% 1Y and +16.55% YTD, with a P/E of 11.38, a 5.50% dividend yield, and very low short interest of 0.20%, offering attractive value and income in the integrated oil space.

Equinor has surged +33.60% YTD and +25% 1Y, with a P/E of 14.50, a 4.74% dividend yield, and positive news sentiment of 0.285, while securing a $1B contract for harsh-environment rigs that underscores its operational strength.

previous_picks.rationale: Valero leads the universe with +59.26% YTD and +93% 1-year return, 1W momentum of +6.88%, P/E 19.01, and a 1.84% dividend yield, with strong refining margins driving outperformance.
Marathon Petroleum shows +56.19% YTD and +53% 1-year return, 1W momentum of +2.88%, P/E 16.83, and positive news sentiment of 0.452, supported by robust refining spreads.
NextEra Energy has +5.68% 1M momentum, P/E 22.28, a 2.84% dividend yield, near-zero short interest of 0.01%, and a 'buy' analyst consensus, benefiting from stable power demand and regulatory support.
SSE shows +4.19% 1W and +5.55% 1M momentum, P/E 23.2, 2.82% dividend yield, with a 'buy' rating, gaining from UK grid investments and renewables expansion.
EOG Resources delivers +23.04% YTD, P/E 12.76, 3.14% dividend yield, and a positive news sentiment of 0.344, though short interest at 3.87% and 1W decline of -3.83% temper the outlook.

Shell trades at a low P/E of 12.08 with a 4.03% dividend yield, positive news sentiment of 0.251, and acts on asset sales ($1.7B Gulf stakes, $1B SA stations) while maintaining 'buy' consensus despite -9.21% 1M slide.

ExxonMobil has +12.98% YTD, low beta of 0.149, P/E 23.06, and a 3.01% dividend yield, but faces headwinds from -8.48% 1M return and falling oil prices due to Iran deal, per [aol.com](https://www.aol.com/finance/exxon-chevron-investors-cautious-oil-113700509.html).
ConocoPhillips shows +9.15% YTD, P/E 17.62, 3.23% dividend yield, and the best analyst score of 1.73 among peers with news sentiment of 0.287, offset by -5.47% 1W decline and weak oil outlook.

Chevron offers a 4.30% dividend yield with a 39-year growth streak, 'buy' consensus at 1.79 analyst score, but -10.80% 1M and -5.81% 1W drops reflect oil price collapse; Morgan Stanley lowered target to $210 per [insidermonkey.com](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/chevron-cvx-price-target-lowered-at-morgan-stanley-here-is-why-1793059/).
Brookfield Renewable has a 4.22% dividend yield and the highest news sentiment of 0.561, with a 'none' analyst rating but favorable renewable tailwinds, despite -5.18% YTD and P/E missing.
